key_points: Power sharing means distributing power among different groups and institutions
Belgium and Sri Lanka are examples used to explain power sharing
Belgium solved ethnic conflict through power sharing arrangements
Sri Lanka followed majoritarian policies that favored the Sinhala majority
Power sharing is considered the spirit of democracy
examples: Belgium shared power between Dutch-speaking and French-speaking communities
Brussels has equal representation of linguistic groups
Sri Lanka declared Sinhala as the only official language in 1956
Coalition governments are examples of power sharing among political parties
formulas: Horizontal Power Sharing = Power shared among Legislature, Executive and Judiciary
Vertical Power Sharing = Power shared among Central, State and Local governments
exam_tips: Remember the Belgium and Sri Lanka case studies
Understand the difference between prudential and moral reasons
Learn the four forms of power sharing
